content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run emitted JavaScript in subprocesses for startup, migration, signal, health, exit code, stderr, package, and clean-handle evidence.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Emitted Subprocess model · Theory Feed Ts Emitte…, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Emitted Subprocess precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Emitted Subprocess model · Theory Feed Ts Emitte…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 5911 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove loopback origins, disposable PostgreSQL, injected faults, emitted subprocesses, and handle reconciliation; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, entity, date, identity, or runtime-validation case and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Run emitted JavaScript in subprocesses for startup, migration, signal, health, exit code, stderr, package, and clean-handle evidence.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Emitted Subprocess model · Theory Feed Ts Emitte…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Importing TypeScript modules proves Node process behavior.

checks.description: The cumulative typescript portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: feed-ts-emitted-subprocess-19dmpx.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: feed-ts-emitted-subprocess-19dmpx(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_feed_ts_emitted_subprocess_19dmpx\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?integrationCases < 14)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reconciledCases < 13 \|\| reconciledCases > integrationCases)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?faultCases < 7)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?leakedHandles > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
